git.note: A:/codex-router is not a Git repository in this workspace.

task.requestedAction: Inspect routing-policy.yaml and report which policy version is active. Do not modify files.

routedPath.entrypoint: runDesktopTask -> runDesktopDecision -> routeTask -> createDesktopExecutionPlan -> host bridge primitives

plannedPrimitives.reason: read current thread context before dispatch
collect explorer output before final synthesis

bridgeCalls.reason: read current thread context before dispatch
collect explorer output before final synthesis

directCliBaseline.entrypoint: codex exec invoked directly with equivalent read-only prompt
directCliBaseline.reasonNotExecuted: This local smoke isolates router effectiveness from live model/network availability. The command shape is recorded as the bypass baseline.
directCliBaseline.commandPreview: codex -a never exec --json --sandbox read-only --cd A:/codex-router --skip-git-repo-check --ephemeral <prompt omitted>
notes: Direct CLI can be made safe by flags, but it does not automatically consume routing-policy.yaml.
Direct CLI has no router approval decision, no desktop execution plan, and no host bridge primitive sequence unless wrapped by codex-router.

effectiveness.conclusion: codex-router is effective only for calls that enter its SDK/host-client path; direct Codex CLI remains a bypass unless a host makes router entry mandatory.

limits: This smoke does not prove Codex Desktop native app uses codex-router internally.
This smoke does not enforce that every human or host must use codex-router.
This smoke proves the SDK path is effective when a host enters through runDesktopTask/DesktopHostClient.
nextGate: To prove mandatory governance, wire a real host entry so all Codex CLI/Desktop actions enter codex-router first, then repeat this smoke against that host entry.
